title: Lumen Quarterly magazine issue: brand-guideline consistency audit of the laid-out pages against the supplied style guide (marked-up findings report)
one_line_ask: Audit every page of the laid-out Lumen Quarterly issue proof against the supplied Lumen brand style guide and return a marked-up, page-referenced brand-consistency findings report listing each colour / logo / typography / imagery drift and the exact rule it breaks.

issues: Colour chip proportions are unequal (navy chip appears slightly wider than coral, coral slightly wider than paper — minor layout inconsistency)

Paper chip (#F4F1EA) has very low contrast against the white background making its right edge soft/barely visible — acceptable as a source file imperfection but worth flagging for designer to add a hairline border
Overall strip height feels compact relative to a 2000x600px spec but composition is clean and functional

Background is pure white, not off-white/near-white as sometimes expected for realism in source assets; however, this is not a penalizable flaw per brief. No visible AI artifacts, stretching, or composition issues. Proportions and type treatment appear correct. PNG is not 2400x800px (appears square, not wide), which is a minor technical issue for a source asset.
Canvas appears to be approximately 1024x1024px (square) rather than the specified 2400x800px landscape ratio — designer will need to confirm export dimensions
'QUARTERLY' tracking/letterspacing is generous but acceptable as a source asset
Slight optical weight imbalance: 'N' terminal on 'LUMEN' appears marginally heavier than 'L' — within serif rendering tolerance
